channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Justice Department orders investigation of local compliance with Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has ordered federal prosecutors to investigate state or local officials who they believe are interfering with the Trump administration's crackdown on immigration, saying they could face criminal charges, in an apparent warning to the dozens of so-called sanct
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes immigrant detention bill that would be Trump's first law to sign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that requires the detainment of unauthorized immigrants accused of theft and violent crimes, marking the first legislation that President Donald Trump can sign as Congress, with some bipartisan support, swiftly moved in line with
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 160 national security staffers are sent home as the White House aligns its team to Trump’s agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s national security adviser on Wednesday sidelined about 160 National Security Council aides, sending them home while the administration reviews staffing and tries to align it with Trump’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson forms committee to probe Jan. 6, Democrats' original investigation
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday said he will establish a new select subcommittee that will probe the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot — and likely dig into the now-defunct Jan. 6 select committee that was led by Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.) and then-Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.). Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration cancels travel for refugees already cleared to come to America
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Refugees who had been approved to come to the United States before a deadline next week suspending America’s refugee resettlement program have had their travel plans canceled by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies’ efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Danish lawmaker addressing EU tells Trump to ‘f--- off’ over Greenland bid
item.description: President Trump ruffled feathers with his plan to buy Greenland, and a Danish MEP is pushing back in a coarsely worded response put in 'words [he] might understand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump puts higher education on notice for 'dangerous, demeaning, and immoral' DEI teachings
item.description: President Donald Trump is seeking to deter the practice of diversity, equity and inclusion in federally funded higher-education institutions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem who called Trump 'existential threat to democracy' now blocking his nominees
item.description: A Senate Democrat is blocking a streamlined process to vote on Trump's nominees, forcing the Senate to take lengthy procedural votes. This strategy was employed by Republicans while in the minority.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Republicans launch effort to ban Chinese nationals from buying land in US
item.description: Republican senators are pitching legislation that would ban Chinese citizens and companies from buying U.S. land entirely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams: I'm 'at a loss' on how to handle migrants in NYC
item.description: Adams criticized the Biden administration for their border policies in an interview with Tucker Carlson.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Where Donald Trump's executive orders rank historically
item.description: President Donald Trump is on pace to surpass Joe Biden's executive orders signed during his first 100 days in office
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Day One actions reversed Biden-era health policies, including efforts to expand ObamaCare
item.description: President Trump is rolling back Biden healthcare policies, such as expansions to the Affordable Care Act – a move Democrats described as an "attack" on the federal program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pritzker, Chicago officials vow to protect immigrant communities
item.description: Chicago's immigrant community and supporters are speaking out, fearful that families could soon be ripped apart.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient the U.S. government. His executive orders cover issues that range from trade, immigration and U.S. foreign aid to demographic diversity, civil rights and the hiring of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s threat of tariffs and sanctions on Russia over Ukraine may fall flat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump threatened on Wednesday to impose stiff taxes, tariffs and sanctions on Russia if an agreement isn’t reached to end the war in Ukraine, a warning that is likely fall on deaf ears in the Kremlin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth lawyer slams 'flawed and questionable affidavit' from ex-sister-in-law
item.description: Pete Hegseth's lawyer dissected and refuted a new affidavit from the defense secretary nominee's former sister-in-law in a letter to SASC chairman Wicker on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace says she is considering a run for South Carolina governor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina said Wednesday that she is “seriously considering” a gubernatorial run in 2026 and will make a final decision in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats grill Project 2025 co-author over ‘illegal’ Trump funding move
item.description: Democrats on Wednesday grilled Office of Management and Budget (OMB) nominee Russell Vought over recent executive orders made by President Trump they say “illegally” target funding authorized by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to huddle with House GOP moderates at White House
item.description: A trio of moderate House Republicans are set to meet with President Trump at the White House on Wednesday, the latest huddle between the president and different GOP factions as the lower chamber prepares to pass his legislative agenda with its slim majority. Reps. Don Bacon (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom uses LA fires to criticize President Trump's reversal of Biden-era climate emissions standards
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom is attempting to criticize President Donald Trump's environmental executive orders by pointing to the recent deadly fires in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Gavel in or gavel out? DeSantis calls for amendment reform
item.description: There are still a lot of questions about Governor Ron DeSantis' call for a special session in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
